author: Ed Swartz
message:
Scale `MouseScrollUnit::Pixels` to `Line` to handle trackpad scaling on e.g. macOS. (#337)

* Scale MouseScrollUnit::Pixels to `Line` to handle trackpad scaling on macOS.

This makes mouse scroll wheel and trackpad scrolling have a similar "feel".

* Update notes per PR feedback.

author: natepiano
message:
Add support for custom binding sources (#335)

* Add support for custom binding sources

- Add `Binding::Custom` variant to the binding enum.
- Add `BindingSource` trait that returns an `ActionValue` for entities
  carrying `Binding::Custom`.
- Add `App::add_binding_source` and `BindingSourceAppExt`, mirroring the
  existing `add_input_condition` and `add_input_modifier` registration.
- Add `BindingSourceRegistry` resource and `BindingSourceFns` component,
  copying the type-erased dispatch pattern from `ConditionFns`/`ModifierFns`.
- Wire `BindingSourceRegistry` through `ScheduleContexts::setup` so the
  update system's filtered query includes source component IDs.
- Dispatch `Binding::Custom` in the update loop to the source's `read`
  before binding-entity modifiers and conditions run.
- Treat `Binding::Custom` as a no-op in `InputReader::value`,
  `InputReader::ignored`, and `IgnoredInputs::add` since custom sources
  bypass raw-input bookkeeping.
- Re-export `BindingSource` and `BindingSourceAppExt` from the prelude.
- Add `tests/binding_source.rs` with three integration tests covering
  value flow, binding-entity modifier application, and the unstaged-zero
  case.
- Add `examples/custom_binding_source.rs` binding Bevy's `PinchGesture`
  to a `Zoom` action that dollies a camera in a small 3D scene.
- Add changelog entry under `[Unreleased]`.

* Replace BindingSource trait with CustomInputs resource

Custom(&'static str) reads its value from a HashMap resource keyed
by name, instead of via a per-entity component implementing a trait.
Modifiers, conditions, and consumption now work without special casing.

* Apply suggestion from @Shatur

Co-authored-by: Hennadii Chernyshchyk <genaloner@gmail.com>

* Address review feedback on CustomInputs

- Read CustomInputs value before the ignored check so the returned
  dimension matches the stored value.
- Rename `customs` to `custom_inputs` in `IgnoredInputs`.
- Restore original placement of `binding_entity` fetch in `update`.
- Display `Binding::Custom(name)` as just `name`.
- Derive `Deref`/`DerefMut` on `CustomInputs`, drop wrapper methods.
- Document `Binding::Custom` and `CustomInputs` per maintainer wording,
  including system ordering example.
- Remove runnable example; keep the doc example.
- Rename `tests/binding_source.rs` to `tests/custom_input.rs`,
  drop the modifier test, shorten test names.

* Inline CustomInputs into the reader module

Single-struct module folded into src/context/input_reader.rs
alongside ActionSources. Doc link on Binding::Custom updated.

* Use CustomInput IDs for Binding::Custom

Replace the &'static str payload with CustomInput, a Copy newtype
around usize. Obtain identifiers via CustomInput::register_input()
and key the CustomInputs resource by them.

Keeps Binding: Copy and makes the type Deserialize<'de> for any
'de, fixing the reflect+serialize feature combo that was failing
in CI. CustomInput, register_input, and CustomInputs live in a
new context::input_reader::custom submodule.

Also drops a redundant explicit doc link path on Binding::Custom.

* Use bevy::platform AtomicUsize for CustomInput counter

core::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize has no fetch_add on thumbv6m-none-eabi,
which broke the no-std CI job.

* Move register_input onto CustomInputs resource

Replaces the process-wide AtomicUsize with a counter field on
CustomInputs and turns register_input into a &mut self method
that increments the resource's own counter.

* Refine CustomInputs doc comments

Reorder the resource doc so registration is described right
after the write paragraph. Shorten the example heading, move
the PinchId struct declaration to the end of the example, and
reword register_input's summary line.

---------

Co-authored-by: Hennadii Chernyshchyk <genaloner@gmail.com>
